info.title: git-subdir-copy
info.description:
A tool to extract some directory from huge git repository. 
            This tool is a part of git-subdir-tools.
            If you want to merge extracted repository to hige repository, 
            use git-subdir-back.

options.name: --commit-even-if-empty
options.description:
Creates commit even if
empty commit. In default,
if there is no change, the
commit will never be
copied.

options.name: --subdir-repo
options.description:
Required. subdir
repository path or url.

options.name: --branch
options.description:
branch name. if not
specified, all branches
will be copied

options.name: --subdir-repo-desc
options.description:
url or identifier of
subdir repository.

options.name: --rootdir-repo-desc
options.description:
url or identifier of
rootdir repository.

options.name: --dir-in-src
options.description:
Required. the directory
which should be copied.
you can choose two or more
directories but only first
directory which is found
will only be used
